<findings>

[observation] [info] Isolated files — no import edges between 19 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
[observation] [info] Dated analysis files under docs/analysis/ create a useful audit trail but may accumulate stale context over time; a periodic review pass to archive superseded analyses would keep this zone actionable.
[observation] [info] Zero cohesion and zero coupling are correct for a documentation zone — these Markdown files carry no import relationships and are consumed by humans, not build tooling.
[observation] [info] docs/architecture/ documents appear to cover cross-cutting concerns (memory, level systems) that are referenced in CLAUDE.md — confirming these stay in sync with code changes would prevent documentation drift.
[relationship] [warning] docs/architecture/ files are referenced from CLAUDE.md but have no automated freshness check. If architectural decisions change (e.g. the four-tier hierarchy evolves or gateway rules shift), these documents will silently become stale without any tooling signal.
[suggestion] [info] Add a dead-link check (e.g. markdown-link-check in CI) for the docs/ directory. Cross-document references and external URLs will silently 404 as the codebase evolves with no current detection mechanism.
[suggestion] [info] Establish a naming or frontmatter convention that distinguishes evergreen architecture documents from dated audit snapshots. A reader landing in docs/analysis/ cannot tell at a glance whether a file is current policy or a historical record without reading its content.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "Project Documentation" (project-documentation) has at-risk risk (score: 0.50, cohesion: 0.00, coupling: 0.00) — approaching architectural risk thresholds

</findings>

<insights>

- Isolated files — no import edges between 19 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- 19 documentation files spanning analysis/, architecture/, and process/ subdirectories with no code imports — this zone is correctly isolated as pure reference material
- The analysis/ subdirectory contains dated audit documents (e.g. 2026-03-03-refresh-orchestration-memory-analysis.md) suggesting a living audit trail; consider whether older analyses should be archived to reduce noise for new contributors
- Architecture documents like memory-architecture.md and level-system-reference.md are high-value onboarding artifacts — ensure they are linked from the root README or CLAUDE.md so they are discoverable
- Zero cohesion and zero coupling are correct for a documentation zone — these Markdown files carry no import relationships and are consumed by humans, not build tooling.
- Dated analysis files under docs/analysis/ create a useful audit trail but may accumulate stale context over time; a periodic review pass to archive superseded analyses would keep this zone actionable.
- docs/architecture/ documents appear to cover cross-cutting concerns (memory, level systems) that are referenced in CLAUDE.md — confirming these stay in sync with code changes would prevent documentation drift.
- Several documents (memory-architecture.md, level-system-reference.md) are explicitly cross-referenced from CLAUDE.md, creating an implicit human-readable coupling to the codebase. Unlike code imports, this coupling is invisible to static analysis and can silently drift out of sync as implementation evolves.
- docs/architecture/ files are referenced from CLAUDE.md but have no automated freshness check. If architectural decisions change (e.g. the four-tier hierarchy evolves or gateway rules shift), these documents will silently become stale without any tooling signal.
- The three subdirectories (analysis/, architecture/, process/) have different longevity profiles — analysis/ files are time-stamped point-in-time audits, architecture/ files are meant to be evergreen references, process/ files are workflow guides — but the zone treats them identically with no structural signal distinguishing living documents from historical snapshots
- No automated dead-link or reference-integrity check exists for the docs/ tree — external URLs and cross-document references can silently rot without any CI signal
- Establish a naming or frontmatter convention that distinguishes evergreen architecture documents from dated audit snapshots. A reader landing in docs/analysis/ cannot tell at a glance whether a file is current policy or a historical record without reading its content.
- Add a dead-link check (e.g. markdown-link-check in CI) for the docs/ directory. Cross-document references and external URLs will silently 404 as the codebase evolves with no current detection mechanism.

</insights>
